ucs.title: [LLM] LockBit 3.0 (Black) pre-encryption inhibitor chain — bcdedit safe-mode boot + VSS/shadow wipe + recovery disable
ucs.description: Hunts the deterministic recovery-inhibitor sequence executed by LockBit 2.0/3.0/Green binaries (and by affiliate hands-on-keyboard scripts) immediately before the T1486 encryption pass. The combination of bcdedit safeboot + recoveryenabled No + vssadmin/wmic shadowcopy delete + wbadmin catalog delete within minutes on the same host is near-pathognomonic for LockBit / LockBit Black; very few benign workloads chain all of these.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe AND (Processes.process="*safeboot*" OR Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*No*" OR Processes.process="*bootstatuspolicy*ignoreallfailures*")) OR (Processes.process_name=vssadmin.exe AND Processes.process="*delete*shadows*") OR (Processes.process_name=wmic.exe AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name=wbadmin.exe AND Processes.process="*delete*catalog*") OR (Processes.process_name=net.exe AND (Processes.process="*stop*VSS*" OR Processes.process="*stop*Veeam*" OR Processes.process="*stop*MSSQL*")) OR (Processes.process_name=sc.exe AND Processes.process="*config*SQLWriter*disabled*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | bin _time span=15m | stats dc(process_name) AS distinct_inhibitors values(process) AS cmds values(parent_process_name) AS parents by dest user _time | where distinct_inhibitors>=3
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot" or ProcessCommandLine has "recoveryenabled No" or ProcessCommandLine has "bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures"))
    or (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete shadows")
    or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe"     and ProcessCommandLine has "shadowcopy delete")
    or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe"  and ProcessCommandLine has "delete catalog")
    or (FileName =~ "net.exe"      and ProcessCommandLine has "stop" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("VSS","Veeam","MSSQL","SQLWriter","BackupExec","MSExchange"))
    or (FileName =~ "sc.exe"       and ProcessCommandLine has "config" and ProcessCommandLine has "disabled")
| summarize distinct_inhibitors=dcount(FileName), cmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,25), parents=make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName,10) by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, 15m)
| where distinct_inhibitors >= 3
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: LockBit's leaked builders (LockBit 3.0/Black config seen in Sept-2022 leak, confirmed in CISA AA23-075A and AA23-165A) hard-code this exact destruction sequence — bcdedit safeboot network, vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet, wmic shadowcopy delete, wbadmin delete catalog -quiet, plus a hard-coded service kill list (SQLWriter, Veeam, MSExchange…). Conti and BlackCat hit some of these but rarely in

ucs.title: [LLM] LockBit affiliate StealBit / Rclone-to-MEGA double-extortion exfil prior to encryption
ucs.description: Catches the staging-and-exfiltration step performed by LockBit affiliates before detonating the encryptor: either the custom 'StealBit' uploader (small Go/Rust binary writing to LockBit C2 over HTTPS) or, more commonly, Rclone configured for the MEGA backend used in the LockBit affiliate playbook. Hunts the process lineage and the egress to MEGA / file.io / anonfiles infrastructure that LockBit operators repeatedly reuse.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rclone.exe","rc.exe","svchost32.exe","winupd.exe") AND (Processes.process="*mega:*" OR Processes.process="*--config*" OR Processes.process="*--transfers*" OR Processes.process="*--multi-thread-streams*" OR Processes.process="*--no-check-certificate*" OR Processes.process="*copy*" OR Processes.process="*sync*")) OR (Processes.process="*StealBit*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest AS src | join type=left src [| tstats summariesonly=true sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) AS bytes_out values(All_Traffic.dest) AS egress_dst from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category="external" (All_Traffic.dest="*mega.nz" OR All_Traffic.dest="*mega.co.nz" OR All_Traffic.dest="*userstorage.mega.co.nz" OR All_Traffic.dest="*file.io" OR All_Traffic.dest="*anonfiles.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*tempsend.com") by All_Traffic.src | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`] | where bytes_out>52428800 | table _time src user process_name process parent_process_name egress_dst bytes_out
ucs.kql:
let exfil_procs = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName in~ ("rclone.exe","rc.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("mega:","--config","--transfers","--multi-thread-streams","--no-check-certificate","copy","sync"))
    or ProcessCommandLine has "StealBit"
    or (FileName matches regex @"^[A-Za-z0-9]{4,10}\.exe$" and FolderPath has_any (@"\Users\Public\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\AppData\Local\Temp\") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("upload","mega.nz","file.io"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let exfil_net = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any ("mega.nz","mega.co.nz","userstorage.mega","file.io","anonfiles.com","tempsend.com","send.exploit.in")
     or RemoteIP in ("31.216.148.0/22","66.203.124.0/24")
| summarize bytes_out=sum(toint(coalesce(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).bytes_sent),"0"))), urls=make_set(RemoteUrl,10), conns=count() by DeviceId, bin(Timestamp, 1h);
exfil_procs
| join kind=inner exfil_net on DeviceId
| where bytes_out > 52428800 or conns > 50
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, urls, bytes_out, conns
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Per CISA AA23-165A, NCSC LockBit advisory, Trend Micro, and SentinelOne affiliate playbooks, LockBit 2.0/3.0 affiliates almost universally exfil with either (a) StealBit — a custom uploader hard-coded to LockBit C2 — or (b) Rclone renamed/dropped to ProgramData with a MEGA remote configured (`mega:`). The combination of an Rclone-style command line with `mega:` and >50 MB egress to mega.nz/usersto
